steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is C, $x^{3}+13x^{2}-5x+28$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Distribute the 4 across the second group
Rewrite with expanded terms and combine like terms
Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
The simplified expression is $x^{3}+13x^{2}-5x+28$.
  • A) $x^{3}+4x^{2}-5x+28$: $x^2$ coefficient is 4, not 13 — incorrect.
  • B) $x^{3}+9x^{2}-5x+28$: $x^2$ coefficient is 9, ignoring the distributed $4x^2$ — incorrect.
  • C) $x^{3}+13x^{2}-5x+28$: matches exactly. That confirms C is correct.
  • D) $x^{3}+7x^{2}-5x+28$: $x^2$ coefficient is 7 — incorrect.
